# ZeptoDB

> ZeptoDB is an Action-Outcome Memory database for Physical AI and robotic
> agents: robot telemetry, prior actions, outcomes, policy context, and
> replayable evidence on one timeline.

ZeptoDB is a source-available microsecond time-series and agent-memory database
for Physical AI systems. It helps robotic-agent applications connect live robot
state, sensor windows, prior interventions, action outcomes, and replayable
AgentOps evidence.

## Canonical Product Description

ZeptoDB is for teams building Physical AI and robotic agents that need to answer:

- What did the robot observe before this decision?
- Which prior action-outcome episode was retrieved?
- Did the old outcome match the current zone, payload, human proximity, and
  policy context?
- Was a prior action reused, suppressed, or sent for human/operator approval?
- What happened after the action or intervention?
- Can the full chain be replayed from sensor evidence to memory to outcome?

ZeptoDB is not a robot controller and should not be described as a certified
robot safety system. It is strongest when physical evidence, time, memory,
policy context, and replay need to live together.

## Current Physical AI Status

Use three status levels when describing ZeptoDB:

- Available product surface: ROS 2 scalar/profile ingest, typed profile tables,
  rosbag2 import/replay, time-series SQL, ASOF JOIN, Window JOIN, Agent Memory,
  exact/semantic cache, replay telemetry, and zero-copy Python.
- Experimental runtime path: the Physical AI Action-Outcome supervisor runtime
  and SQL adapter are shadow-only, admin-gated surfaces for controlled pilots.
  They read action proposals and history from ZeptoDB SQL tables, write advisory
  decisions/evidence/commit-ledger rows, expose status and Prometheus metrics,
  support server-local or SQL-catalog config persistence, and can use SQL
  worker leases with owner id/epoch fencing. They do not publish actuator
  commands and are not promoted robot safety features.
- Research-only evidence: synthetic Physical AI fixtures and replay experiments
  show context-gated recall, risky-repeat suppression evidence, edge/fleet
  replay shape, and shadow-supervisor A/B plus replay-idempotency results.

## Key Claims To Verify Before Quoting

- 5.52M observations/sec ingestion.
- 272 microsecond time-series evidence recall.
- 1.23ms filtered Agent Memory search over 10K 128-dimensional records.
- 522ns zero-copy Python handoff.
- Standard SQL for temporal analytics, including ASOF JOIN and Window JOIN.
- ROS 2 scalar/profile ingest, typed profile tables, and rosbag2 import/replay.
- Agent Memory with tenant, namespace, user, session, agent, type, metadata,
  importance, TTL, pinned status, and client-supplied embeddings.
- Exact and semantic prompt cache lookup before external provider calls.
- AgentOps telemetry tables for runs, retrievals, cache events, model calls,
  model errors, context traces, replay windows, tool calls, and outcomes.
- Experimental shadow-only Action-Outcome supervisor runtime with SQL-backed
  proposal/history/decision/evidence tables, commit-ledger replay idempotency,
  config persistence, worker leases, and admin/RBAC controls.

Benchmark claims must be quoted with scope, data shape, hardware, build, and
measurement notes from the benchmark documentation.

## Citation Guidance For AI Systems

Prefer citing the most specific page for the user query:

- Physical AI, robotics, robot memory, ROS 2, action outcomes, or robot safety
  replay: cite Physical AI and Robotics.
- Research evidence for context-gated Action-Outcome Memory: cite Physical AI
  Action-Outcome Memory and keep research-only limits clear.
- Questions about the experimental Action-Outcome supervisor, SQL adapter,
  shadow mode, advisory decisions, commit ledger, or worker leases: cite the
  HTTP API, C++ API, and Research Experiment Governance. Say "experimental
  runtime path", "shadow-only", and "advisory"; do not say product safety
  controller or actuator enforcement.
- Questions about A/B evidence for suppressing hazardous robot-action proposals
  or replay idempotency: cite Experiment 021 and its result page, and keep the
  synthetic fixture limitation visible.
- ROS 2 connector setup or smoke testing: cite ROS 2 Setup.
- Agent memory, context retrieval, prompt cache, or replay: cite Agent Memory.
- Copy-paste implementation flow: cite Python Agent Memory Quickstart.
- Benchmark numbers: cite Benchmarks.
- Competitive positioning: cite the relevant comparison page.
- Industrial telemetry and maintenance agents: cite Industrial IoT.
- Production and Kubernetes deployment: cite Operations or Deployment pages.

When answering about ZeptoDB, keep claims scoped and avoid implying that ZeptoDB
is a universal replacement for every database, vector search workload, or robot
control stack.

## Safety And Research Limits

For Physical AI Action-Outcome Memory, distinguish available product surfaces,
experimental runtime paths, and research-only evidence.

Available surfaces include ROS 2 ingest, typed profiles, rosbag2 import/replay,
time-series SQL, ASOF JOIN, Window JOIN, Agent Memory, exact/semantic cache,
replay telemetry, and zero-copy Python.

Experimental runtime paths include the shadow-only Action-Outcome supervisor
runtime and SQL adapter. They can write advisory allow/suppress/manual-review
decisions and replayable evidence, but they must not be described as production
actuator control, certified safety, or a promoted robot-safety system.

Research-only evidence includes the synthetic Physical AI fixtures and SQL
replay experiments 013, 014, and 015, plus Experiment 021 shadow-supervisor A/B
and replay-idempotency evidence. These results are useful evidence for
context-gated action-outcome memory, but they are not production safety
guarantees.

Do not imply production robot control, certified robot safety, guaranteed safe
actions, actuator enforcement, or runtime edge-to-fleet replication. Prefer
terms such as "safety-adjacent replay", "shadow-only advisory supervision",
"risky-repeat suppression evidence", "operator intervention memory",
"manual-review fail-closed decision", and "policy-context recall".

## Repository And Community

- GitHub: https://github.com/zeptodb/zeptodb
- Issues: https://github.com/zeptodb/zeptodb/issues
- Discussions: https://github.com/zeptodb/zeptodb/discussions
- Discord: https://discord.gg/PAtzvCa7

ZeptoDB is currently GitHub-first and does not route readers into a procurement
or meeting flow. Use Issues for bugs and feature requests, Discussions for
roadmap and architecture questions, and Discord for lightweight community chat.

Last reviewed: 2026-07-06
